Test_Objective: Verify language dropdown is visible on the Login page
Steps:
1. Navigate to /login.
2. Observe the header area.
Expected_Result: Language dropdown is displayed in the header with Vietnam flag icon and "VN" text.

Test_Objective: Verify language dropdown is visible on the Home page for authenticated users
Precondition: User is logged in with valid session
Steps:
1. Log in successfully.
2. Navigate to home page.
3. Observe the header area.
Expected_Result: Language dropdown is displayed in the header with current language flag and code.

Test_Objective: Verify language dropdown is consistently available across all application pages
Steps:
1. Navigate to home page and verify dropdown is present.
2. Navigate to kudos page and verify dropdown is present.
3. Navigate to awards page and verify dropdown is present.
Expected_Result: Language dropdown is displayed in the header on all pages with consistent appearance.

Test_Objective: Verify language dropdown remains visible when session expires
Steps:
1. Log in successfully.
2. Wait for session to expire (or manually expire the token).
3. Refresh the page.
4. Observe the header area.
Expected_Result: Language dropdown is still visible on the redirected login page.

Test_Objective: Verify language dropdown is reachable via keyboard Tab navigation
Precondition: User is on any page with the language dropdown
Steps:
1. Navigate to any page.
2. Press Tab key repeatedly to cycle through focusable elements.
Expected_Result: Language dropdown receives focus and shows a visible focus indicator.

Test_Objective: Verify the dropdown displays correctly in its default collapsed state
Precondition: User is on a page with the language dropdown
Steps:
1. Navigate to any page.
2. Observe the language dropdown in the header.
Expected_Result: Dropdown displays: Vietnam flag icon on the left, "VN" text on the right, dark gray background. Compact and properly aligned in header.

Test_Objective: Verify the dropdown displays correctly when expanded
Precondition: User is on a page with the language dropdown
Steps: 1. Click on the language dropdown.
Expected_Result: Dropdown expands showing: Selected item (VN) on top with dark gray background, EN option below with darker/black background. UK flag + "EN" text displayed in option area.

Test_Objective: Verify the Vietnam flag icon renders correctly
Steps: 1. Observe the VN option in the dropdown.
Expected_Result: Vietnam flag (red background with yellow star) is displayed as a small rectangular icon. Clearly recognizable.

Test_Objective: Verify the UK/British flag icon renders correctly
Steps:
1. Click on the language dropdown.
2. Observe the EN option.
Expected_Result: UK flag (Union Jack) is displayed as a small rectangular icon on the left side of "EN" text. Clearly recognizable.

Test_Objective: Verify text is readable with proper contrast against dark background
Steps:
1. Observe VN text in collapsed state.
2. Open dropdown and observe EN text.
Expected_Result: Both "VN" and "EN" text are displayed in white color with sufficient contrast against the dark backgrounds.

Test_Objective: Verify hover visual feedback on the EN language option
Steps:
1. Click to open the dropdown.
2. Hover mouse over the EN option.
Expected_Result: EN option background changes to a highlight color to indicate interactive state. Cursor changes to pointer.

Test_Objective: Verify hover visual feedback on the VN language option when not selected
Precondition: Current language is EN and dropdown is expanded
Steps:
1. Switch language to EN first.
2. Click to open the dropdown.
3. Hover mouse over the VN option.
Expected_Result: VN option background changes to indicate hover state. Cursor changes to pointer.

Test_Objective: Verify visual difference between selected and non-selected language
Steps:
1. Click to open the dropdown.
2. Compare the selected language item vs the non-selected option.
Expected_Result: Selected language (top area) has a distinct dark gray background. Non-selected language (bottom area) has a darker/black background. Clear visual distinction between the two states.

Test_Objective: Verify the EN option item matches design dimensions
Steps:
1. Open dropdown.
2. Inspect EN option element dimensions.
Expected_Result: EN option measures approximately 110x56px as per design spec.

Test_Objective: Verify dropdown displays correctly on mobile viewport
Steps:
1. Set viewport to 320px width.
2. Navigate to any page.
3. Observe language dropdown.
Expected_Result: Language dropdown is visible, properly sized for mobile, touch target at least 44x44px. Flag and text are not cut off.

Test_Objective: Verify dropdown displays correctly on tablet viewport
Steps:
1. Set viewport to 768px width.
2. Navigate to any page.
3. Observe language dropdown.
Expected_Result: Language dropdown displays correctly, properly positioned in header, all elements visible.

Test_Objective: Verify dropdown displays correctly on desktop viewport
Precondition: Browser viewport set to 1024px or wider
Steps:
1. Set viewport to 1024px width.
2. Navigate to any page.
3. Observe language dropdown.
Expected_Result: Language dropdown displays correctly at top-right of header, matching Figma design specifications.

Test_Objective: Verify dropdown is positioned correctly within the page header
Steps:
1. Navigate to any page.
2. Observe dropdown position within header.
Expected_Result: Dropdown is positioned at the top-right area of the header, properly aligned with other header elements.

Test_Objective: Verify visible focus indicator when dropdown receives keyboard focus
Steps:
1. Use Tab key to navigate to the dropdown.
2. Observe the focus state.
Expected_Result: A visible focus ring/outline is displayed around the dropdown when it receives keyboard focus.

Test_Objective: Verify dropdown updates its display after switching to English
Steps:
1. Open dropdown.
2. Click EN option.
3. Observe collapsed dropdown.
Expected_Result: Collapsed dropdown now shows UK flag + "EN" text. Previously displayed VN flag is replaced.

Test_Objective: Verify clicking the dropdown opens the language menu
Steps: 1. Click on the language dropdown.
Expected_Result: Dropdown expands showing both VN and EN options. Selected language is visually distinguished from the non-selected option.

Test_Objective: Verify clicking the dropdown again closes the menu
Steps:
1. Click on the language dropdown to open it.
2. Click on the dropdown trigger again.
Expected_Result: Dropdown collapses. Current language selection remains unchanged.

Test_Objective: Verify clicking outside the dropdown closes the menu
Steps:
1. Click on the language dropdown to open it.
2. Click on any area outside the dropdown.
Expected_Result: Dropdown collapses. Current language selection remains unchanged.

Test_Objective: Verify selecting EN changes the application language to English
Steps:
1. Click on the language dropdown.
2. Click on the EN option.
Expected_Result: Dropdown closes. Dropdown display updates to show UK flag + "EN". Application UI text changes to English.

Test_Objective: Verify selecting VN changes the application language to Vietnamese
Steps:
1. Click on the language dropdown.
2. Click on the VN option.
Expected_Result: Dropdown closes. Dropdown display updates to show Vietnam flag + "VN". Application UI text changes to Vietnamese.

Test_Objective: Verify selecting the already-active language does not cause errors
Steps:
1. Click on the language dropdown.
2. Click on the VN option (already selected).
Expected_Result: Dropdown closes. Language remains VN. No errors or visual glitches occur.

Test_Objective: Verify selected language persists after reloading the page
Precondition: User has switched language to EN
Steps:
1. Switch language to EN.
2. Reload the page (F5).
Expected_Result: After reload dropdown shows EN and UI remains in English.

Test_Objective: Verify selected language persists when navigating between pages
Precondition: User has switched language to EN
Steps:
1. Switch language to EN on the current page.
2. Navigate to a different page (e.g. kudos page).
3. Observe language dropdown and page content.
Expected_Result: Dropdown shows EN on the new page. UI content is displayed in English.

Test_Objective: Verify switching language back and forth works correctly
Steps:
1. Switch to EN.
2. Verify UI is in English.
3. Switch back to VN.
4. Verify UI is in Vietnamese.
5. Switch to EN again.
6. Verify UI is in English.
Expected_Result: Each switch correctly updates the dropdown display and application UI language without errors.

Test_Objective: Verify dropdown can be opened via keyboard Enter key
Steps:
1. Tab to the language dropdown.
2. Press Enter key.
Expected_Result: Dropdown expands showing language options.

Test_Objective: Verify arrow keys navigate between language options
Steps:
1. Open dropdown with Enter.
2. Press Down arrow key.
3. Press Up arrow key.
Expected_Result: Down arrow highlights the next option. Up arrow highlights the previous option. Highlighted option is visually indicated.

Test_Objective: Verify pressing Enter selects the highlighted language option
Precondition: Dropdown is expanded, an option is highlighted
Steps:
1. Open dropdown.
2. Use arrow keys to highlight EN.
3. Press Enter.
Expected_Result: EN is selected. Dropdown closes. Language updates to English.

Test_Objective: Verify pressing Escape closes the dropdown without changing selection
Steps:
1. Open dropdown.
2. Press Escape key.
Expected_Result: Dropdown closes. Language selection remains unchanged.

Test_Objective: Verify switching language does not affect authentication state
Steps:
1. Log in to the application.
2. Switch language from VN to EN.
3. Verify user is still authenticated.
Expected_Result: User remains logged in after language switch. No re-authentication required.

Test_Objective: Verify dropdown works correctly with touch interactions on mobile
Precondition: User is on a mobile device or emulated touch device
Steps:
1. Tap on the language dropdown.
2. Tap on EN option.
Expected_Result: Dropdown opens on first tap. Language switches to EN on second tap. Dropdown closes after selection.

Test_Objective: Verify rapid clicking does not cause inconsistent state
Steps: 1. Rapidly click on the dropdown toggle multiple times.
Expected_Result: Dropdown opens and closes correctly without getting stuck in an inconsistent state.

Test_Objective: Verify changing language value via dev tools does not affect server-side behavior
Steps:
1. Open browser dev tools.
2. Inspect the dropdown element.
3. Change the language value attribute to an invalid value.
4. Attempt to submit or navigate.
Expected_Result: Application behavior is unaffected by the client-side manipulation. Server uses validated language value.

Test_Objective: Verify adding a new language option via dev tools does not affect functionality
Steps:
1. Open browser dev tools.
2. Add a new option element (e.g. "FR") to the dropdown.
3. Select the new option.
Expected_Result: Application ignores the invalid option. Only VN and EN are accepted as valid language selections.

Test_Objective: Verify language preference is remembered after closing and reopening browser
Steps:
1. Switch language to EN.
2. Close the browser completely.
3. Reopen browser and navigate to the application.
Expected_Result: Application loads with EN language (or falls back to default VN based on cookie/storage implementation).

Test_Objective: Verify the default language is VN on first visit
Precondition: User visits application for the first time (no cookies/storage)
Steps:
1. Clear all browser data for the application.
2. Navigate to the application.
Expected_Result: Language dropdown shows Vietnam flag + "VN". Application UI is displayed in Vietnamese.
